name: Gorgon CO2 Injection Project
source: IEEFA, Chevron, WA DMPE
notes: Dupuy Fm saline aquifer; FY2024-25 captured 1.33 Mt, only ~25-30% of CO2 removed from gas stream due to reservoir pressure constraints. Underperforming.

name: Santos Moomba CCS
source: Santos, SPE JPT, CO2CRC
notes: Depleted Cooper Basin gas reservoirs; passed 2 Mt mid-2026; Phase 2 (JX/ENEOS) targets 5 Mtpa by 2028 incl. third-party Asian CO2.

name: CarbonNet Project (Pelican/Kookaburra)
source: djsir.vic.gov.au
notes: Two permitted offshore sites, combined >350 Mt; Stage 3 Project Development & Commercial Establishment.

name: South West Hub (Collie CCS Hub)
source: WA CCUS Action Plan, zeroco2.no
notes: Triassic Lesueur Sandstone near Collie; stalled for years, revived under WA 2024 CCUS Action Plan. Appraisal/paused.

name: CO2CRC Otway Project
source: CO2CRC, CSLF
notes: Research/MMV facility; >13 years of monitoring research; Stage 3 recognized by CSLF 2025.

name: Bayu-Undan CCS
operator: Santos, SK E&S, INPEX, Eni, Tokyo Timor Sea Resources
source: Santos, Offshore Magazine, Tatoli
notes: Repurposing depleted Bayu-Undan gas field; production ceased 2025; FEED near-complete, FID target 2025-26, injection from 2028; ~US$1.6bn capex.

name: Tomakomai CCS Demonstration
source: JOGMEC, METI
notes: Demo injected ~300 kt 2016-2019; area re-selected in FY2024 JOGMEC Advanced CCS Projects (JAPEX/Idemitsu/Hokkaido Electric) targeting ~1.5-2 Mtpa.

name: Metropolitan Area (Kanto) Advanced CCS Project
operator: INPEX, Nippon Steel, Kanto Natural Gas Development
source: JOGMEC, Nippon Steel, Rigzone
notes: FY2024 JOGMEC Advanced CCS Project; FY2025 contract extended for engineering/appraisal drilling.

name: Offshore Western Kyushu / Setouchi Advanced CCS Project
source: JOGMEC, MHI
notes: FY2024 Advanced CCS Project; CO2 from Setouchi/Kyushu refineries and thermal plants.

name: Niigata Advanced CCS Project
source: JOGMEC FY2024
notes: Among 9 FY2024 Advanced CCS Projects; volumes not yet public.

name: Japan-Malaysia (Setouchi to Sarawak) Cross-border CCS
operator: JAPEX, Mitsubishi UBE Cement, Setouchi consortium
source: JAPEX, JOGMEC
notes: Cross-border: Setouchi industrial CO2 shipped to offshore Sarawak; 1.9-2.9 Mtpa target; FY2024 JOGMEC Advanced CCS list.

name: CNOOC Enping 15-1 Offshore CCS
source: CNOOC, SCMP, China Daily
notes: China's first offshore CCS demo; saline formation ~800m below seabed, ~190km SW of Hong Kong; >100M m3 injected by 2025; Phase 2 expansion under study.

name: China Energy (Guohua) Ordos CCUS Demonstration
source: China Energy, ScienceDirect
notes: Full-chain demo into deep saline formations; ~300 kt cumulative; distinct from Guohua Jinjie EOR (excluded).

name: Huaneng Gansu Zhengning CCUS
source: SASAC, ScienceDirect Sept 2025
notes: World's largest coal-fired capture demo (1.5 Mtpa), operational Sept 2025; storage split geologic/EOR/green-fuel still emerging — verify dedicated-storage share.

name: Sinopec Shengli Offshore CCS Phase 2 (Bohai)
source: Sinopec; ScienceDirect
notes: Phase 2 concept: post-combustion capture to offshore saline storage; distinct from onshore Qilu-Shengli EOR (excluded).

name: China Resources Haifeng CCUS (Guangdong)
source: UK-China Guangdong CCUS Centre
notes: Long-stalled pre-feasibility; offshore saline storage under evaluation.

name: Korea Donghae-1 CCS
source: KNOC, SLB, SCMP
notes: Korea's first large-scale CCS; repurposed depleted Donghae-1 gas field (2004-2021); 1.2 Mtpa target 2025-2030, schedule slipping (still pre-FEED).

name: Petronas Kasawari CCS
source: NS Energy, The Star, Borneo Post
notes: One of the largest offshore CCS projects regionally; gas production began Aug 2024; injection accelerated to 2027.

name: Petronas Lang Lebah CCS
source: Khor Reports
notes: High CO2/H2S field; EPC bids over budget early 2025, in re-engineering; target 2028.

name: Petronas CCS Ventures Offshore Assessment
source: Petronas 2025
notes: Malaysia's first offshore CO2 storage assessment permit; RINA FEED contract Oct 2025; ASEAN storage-hub strategy.

name: BP Tangguh UCC (Ubadari CCUS & Compression)
source: Hart Energy, BP
notes: FID Nov 2024, $7bn; up to 15 Mt CO2 over field life; production start ~2028.

name: ExxonMobil/Pertamina Sunda-Asri CCS Hub
source: ICIS, Jakarta Globe
notes: Up to 3 Gt potential in Sunda-Asri basins; ExxonMobil committed up to $15bn incl. petrochemical complex; A&R HoA signed 2026.

name: ONGC Gandhar CCS Pilot
source: Down to Earth 2025-26
notes: India's first full-scale CCS pilot; ~100 t/day into two depleted wells at Gandhar oilfield; also tests EOR — verify dedicated-storage share.

name: NTPC Pakri Barwadih CO2 Storage Appraisal
source: downtoearth.org.in 2025
notes: India's first dedicated geological CO2 storage appraisal borewell (~1,200m, late 2025); pre-injection.

name: PTTEP Arthit CCS
source: PTTEP, Bangkok Post
notes: Thailand's first CCS; FID Sept 2025; depleted Arthit gas reservoirs 1,000-2,000m; ~$320M over 5 yr; Mitsui participating.

name: CPC Tiezhenshan CCS Trial
source: SLB, Taipei Times
notes: Taiwan's first approved CCS (EIA passed 2023); 300 kt over 3 years, monitored 2028-2030.

name: Kapuni Gas Field CCS (prospective)
source: MBIE
notes: Prime NZ candidate pending CCS legislation (expected 2026); no sanctioned project yet.

name: Carbfix Hellisheiði (CarbFix1/CarbFix2 + Orca/Mammoth DAC injection)
source: Carbfix.com; Matter et al. 2016 Science; Wikipedia Carbfix
notes: World's first and longest-running dedicated ISM site. CarbFix1 pilot began 2012 (geothermal-plant CO2/H2S co-injected dissolved in water into basalt at ~400-800m, Hellisheiði geothermal plant); CarbFix2 scaled to capture ~12,000 t CO2/yr from the plant since 2014. Also hosts injection for Climeworks' Orca (4kt/yr, since 2021) and Mammoth (36kt/yr capacity, since 2024) DAC plants. >95% mineralization within ~2 years. Cumulative Carbfix-wide (geothermal + DAC) injected ~110,000+ t CO2 by 2025-26.

name: Carbfix Helguvík / CO2 Seastone (seawater dissolution pilot)
source: Carbfix.com 'Carbfix tests using seawater to mineralize CO2 at Helguvík'
notes: World's first injection of CO2 dissolved in seawater (rather than freshwater) into basaltic subsurface, ~400m depth below sea level, Reykjanesbær. Tests imported CO2 (from Switzerland) as a precursor/proof-of-concept for the Coda Terminal import model.

name: Carbfix Coda Terminal (Straumsvík CO2 import & storage hub)
source: Carbfix.com Coda Terminal page; EU Innovation Fund award; Carbfix 2026 guidance
notes: Large-scale hub to import captured CO2 by ship (from Europe, incl. Switzerland/Dan-Unity partners) and permanently mineralize it in basalt at Straumsvík near Reykjavik. First commercial imported-CO2 injection targeted mid-2026 (~500,000 t/yr with one ship); full capacity of 3 Mtpa (5 ships) targeted by 2031. On-site 'Silverstone' capture/receiving infrastructure under construction 2025-26.

name: Carbfix Nesjavellir Geothermal Reinjection
source: Carbfix.com operations overview
notes: Second Carbfix geothermal-plant reinjection site (Nesjavellir geothermal power station), smaller scale than Hellisheiði; part of Carbfix's four current Icelandic injection sites (Hellisheiði, Nesjavellir, Orca, Helguvík).

name: Reykjanes Ridge Sub-Seabed Basalt Pilot
source: ScienceDirect 2024 'Initial results of a pilot project for sub-seabed basalt storage of carbon dioxide on the Reykjanes Ridge'
notes: First sub-seabed basalt CO2 storage pilot on an active mid-ocean ridge segment offshore SW Iceland, extending the onshore Carbfix approach offshore; initial results reported 2024.

name: 44.01 Oman Original Pilot (Fizh/Samail massif)
source: JPT/SPE 2023 coverage; XPRIZE Carbon Removal 2023 winner profile
notes: 44.01's original demonstration injecting CO2 dissolved in water into peridotite of the Samail ophiolite, proving <12-month mineralization; basis for the $1M XPRIZE Carbon Removal milestone award and subsequent Project Hajar scale-up.

name: 44.01 Project Hajar (Al Qabil, Hajar Mountains commercial-scale)
operator: 44.01 (with AirCapture DAC, Oman Ministry of Energy and Minerals)
source: 44.01.earth Project Hajar blog; Times of Oman; XPRIZE finalist profile
notes: World's first concession for commercial-scale peridotite mineralization, signed with Oman's Ministry of Energy and Minerals at Al Qabil, Hajar Mountains. First borehole drilled and injectivity/safety tests completed 2023-24; co-located AirCapture modular DAC units (up to 500 t CO2/yr each). XFACTOR $1M award winner. Full commercial buildout still in development as of mid-2026; coordinates approximate (Hajar Mountains/Sharqiyah region).

name: 44.01 / ADNOC Fujairah Pilot
operator: 44.01 / ADNOC / Fujairah Natural Resources Corporation / Masdar
source: ADNOC press releases 2023, 2024; Carbon Herald; Gulf News Oct 2024
notes: Pilot mineralized 10 tonnes of DAC-captured CO2 (dissolved in seawater) into peridotite in under 100 days (2023-24), UAE's first carbon-to-rock demonstration.

name: 44.01 / ADNOC Fujairah Scale-Up (Phase 1)
source: ADNOC press release Nov 2024 'ADNOC and 44.01 to Scale Up Carbon-to-Rock Project'; AGBI
notes: Scale-up phase targeting >300 tonnes CO2 injected over a longer duration, demonstrating pathway to larger deployment; part of ADNOC's 10 Mtpa 2030 carbon capture target. Status as of mid-2026: scale-up phase underway/ramping.

name: Octavia Carbon / Cella Project Hummingbird (Lake Elementaita, Kenya Rift)
source: decarbonfuse.com May 2026; Carbon Herald; XPRIZE Octavia & Cella profile
notes: First DAC+geologic mineral storage project in the Southern Hemisphere / Africa. First injection test May 2026: ~460 kg CO2 injected into basalt at ~9 kg/min using water-alternating-gas (WAG) technique adapted from EOR, near Lake Elementaita, Central Rift Valley. Targeting ~1,000 net tonnes removed/yr by end-2026 via 68 modular DAC units, powered by geothermal-adjacent grid.

name: Great Carbon Valley / Climeworks Kenya DAC+S (planned)
source: Climeworks press release; MIT Technology Review Dec 2025 'Welcome to Kenya's Great Carbon Valley'
notes: Nairobi-based systems integrator Great Carbon Valley partnering with Climeworks to develop DAC+S projects targeting up to 1 Mt CO2/yr removal via basalt mineralization in Kenya's Rift Valley, leveraging geothermal power (Olkaria/Menengai). Site selection and early development stage as of mid-2026; coordinates approximate (Rift Valley geothermal belt).

name: Ankeron Pacific Northwest DAC & Carbon Mineralization Hub
operator: RMI (lead), Carbfix, PNNL, Heirloom, Removr, Sustaera + 13-org consortium
source: DOE FOA 2735 award; RMI press release; SSRN Ankeron Hub paper 2024
notes: DOE-funded ($3M) feasibility/design study for a regional DAC + Columbia River Basalt mineral storage hub in eastern Washington/Pacific Northwest, combining multiple DAC technology developers with Carbfix/PNNL basalt-injection expertise. Feasibility study stage as of mid-2026; no injection yet. Coordinates approximate (Columbia River Basalt region near Wallula).

name: Deep Sky / Carbfix Thetford Mines Pilot Injection
source: Newswire 'Deep Sky completes Quebec's first geological injection of captured carbon'; Deep Sky blog
notes: Quebec's first geological CO2 injection using the Carbfix mineralization method, at the historic Thetford Mines asbestos/serpentinite belt. First North American mineralization injection by Deep Sky. Companion pre-feasibility study underway at Bécancour industrial park (SPIPB) for a larger sedimentary/mineral storage site.

name: CO2 Lock SAM Project (Brucite-Serpentinite, Central BC)
source: FPX Nickel/CO2 Lock press release Jan 2024; Mining Stock Education
notes: First-ever successful CO2 injection into brucite-rich ultramafic (serpentinized peridotite) rock, at the SAM property in central British Columbia, near FPX Nickel's Decar Ni project. Builds on ~decade of Decar tailings mineralogy research. Field program completed; further site characterization ongoing across BC ultramafic belts (Decar, Cassiar, Shulaps, Atlin).

name: PNNL Wallula Basalt Pilot
operator: PNNL / Big Sky Carbon Sequestration Partnership (Boise White Paper Mill site)
source: PNNL Wallula Basalt Project page; McGrail et al. 2017 Environ. Sci. Technol.; ResearchGate 'Quantification of CO2 Mineralization at the Wallula Basalt Pilot Project'
notes: DOE Regional Carbon Sequestration Partnership Phase II pilot; ~977 t (~1 kt) CO2 injected in 2013 into Columbia River Basalt Group interflow zones near Wallula, WA. Post-injection coring (2015-2016) found up to 60% of injected carbon mineralized as ankerite within ~2 years, demonstrating basalt ISM feasibility at pilot scale. Concluded; foundational for later Ankeron Hub and Cascadia CarbonSAFE offshore studies.

name: Sleipner CO2 Storage
source: Global CCS Institute; Equinor; IEEFA 2023
notes: Utsira Sand saline aquifer, ~800-1000m depth. First industrial-scale dedicated CO2 storage project globally. >26 Mt cumulative by 2026 at ~1.0 Mtpa.

name: Snøhvit CO2 Storage
source: Global CCS Institute; Equinor
notes: CO2 from Snøhvit LNG plant injected into Tubåen/Stø Fm saline aquifer, Barents Sea. Storage zone changed from Tubåen to Stø Fm in 2011 after pressure issues.

name: Northern Lights CCS Phase 1
source: Equinor Aug 2025 'First CO2 volumes stored at Northern Lights'; norlights.com
notes: First CO2 receipt/injection from Heidelberg Materials Brevik cement plant began Aug 2025, ~2,600m below seabed, Johansen Fm, North Sea. Phase 1 capacity 1.5 Mtpa.

name: Northern Lights CCS Phase 2 Expansion
source: Equinor Mar 2025 press release; JPT Apr 2025
notes: NOK 7.5bn investment approved Mar 2025 to expand total injection capacity from 1.5 to at least 5 Mtpa; new customer Stockholm Exergi; expected ready H2 2028.

name: Quest CCS (Shell Scotford, Alberta)
source: Shell; Global CCS Institute
notes: Captures CO2 from Scotford Upgrader hydrogen production, injects into Basal Cambrian Sands saline aquifer ~2km depth near Thornhill, AB via 3 wells. >11 Mt cumulative by 2026.

name: Illinois Industrial CCS (ADM Decatur)
source: EPA enforcement order 2024-25; E&E News 'Carbon storage site that leaked set to restart injections'; sequestration.mit.edu
notes: First commercial-scale saline storage project in the US operating under EPA Class VI permit (effective 2017), injecting CO2 from corn-processing/ethanol into Mt. Simon Sandstone. EPA issued a compliance order in 2024-25 after injected fluid migrated into an unauthorized zone ~5,000 ft below surface (no public health impact); ADM undertaking corrective monitoring, restarting/continuing injections under order as of mid-2026.

name: Broadwing Energy Center CCS (Decatur, IL)
source: IL EPA draft air permit Apr 2026; Global Energy Monitor; Modern Power Systems
notes: 452 MW gas-fired cogeneration plant in Decatur, IL supplying ADM and Google; ~90% of CO2 emissions to be captured and injected into ADM's existing EPA Class VI wells (Mt. Simon Sandstone). Draft air construction permit issued Apr 2026 (comment period to Jun 2026); FID targeted Q2 2026; commercial operation targeted 2030.

name: In Salah CCS (Algeria)
source: IEAGHG; Global CCS Institute
notes: CO2 stripped from produced natural gas injected into Krechba Fm saline sandstone flanking gas field 2004-2011 (~3.8 Mt total); injection suspended in 2011 over caprock integrity/microseismicity concerns; project concluded, long-running monitoring case study.

name: Al Reyadah CCUS (Emirates Steel, Abu Dhabi)
source: ADNOC; CSLF project page; JPT/SPE Habshan coverage
notes: MENA's first commercial CCUS project; captures up to 0.8 Mtpa CO2 from Emirates Steel Arkan, currently used for enhanced oil recovery at ADNOC onshore fields (EOR-adjacent — not pure dedicated storage). ADNOC exploring conversion to a dedicated Transport & Storage Company (T&SCo) model similar to Northern Lights. See also Habshan CCUS (separate entry) for ADNOC's first dedicated geological (non-EOR) storage project.

name: ADNOC Habshan CCUS (Bab Far North Field)
source: ADNOC FID release; Petrofac $615M contract award; Drilling Contractor
notes: ADNOC FID'd 1.5 Mtpa CCUS facility at Habshan, Al Gharbia; CO2 to be injected and permanently stored (dedicated geological storage, not EOR) in the Bab Far North Field. Would triple ADNOC's capture capacity to 2.3 Mtpa. Commissioning targeted 2026; largest CCUS project in MENA at FID.

name: Porthos CCS (Netherlands)
source: Porthos.co.nl schedule revision 2026; CO2-View
notes: Depleted gas field ~20km offshore Rotterdam; construction of pipeline/platform ongoing; deep-sea injection well retrofits completed 2025-26; commercial start delayed from originally-planned 2026 to not before H2 2027 due to interdependent construction/supply-chain delays.

name: Aramis CCS (Netherlands / North Sea)
source: Seed data; Global CCS Institute
notes: Open-access CO2 transport & storage hub targeting depleted North Sea gas fields; larger successor to Porthos; FID and permitting progressing 2025-26, targeted start ~2028.

name: Greensand Future CCS (Denmark)
operator: INEOS E&P / Harbour Energy / Danish North Sea Fund
source: Carbon Herald; NS Energy; EU CINEA project fiche
notes: Depleted Nini West oil field, Danish North Sea. 2023 pilot injected ~4,100 t CO2 shipped from INEOS Antwerp (world's first cross-border offshore CCS value chain). Full storage license approved; ramp-up to commercial operation from mid-2026 at initial 0.4 Mtpa, expanding to 4-8 Mtpa by 2030 — set to be Denmark's and the EU's first full-scale operational CO2 storage site.

name: Acorn CCS (Scotland / North Sea)
source: Seed data; UK Track-1 expansion process
notes: St Fergus terminal, injecting into depleted Goldeneye gas field/Captain Sandstone, North Sea. Part of UK's Track-2/expansion CCS cluster process; funding negotiations and FEED ongoing through 2025-26.

name: Northern Endurance Partnership / Endurance (East Coast Cluster, UK)
source: UK government East Coast Cluster Track-1 funding; NEP updates
notes: Endurance saline aquifer, Southern North Sea, off Teesside/Humber. UK's first Track-1 CCS cluster (East Coast Cluster, incl. Net Zero Teesside, H2Teesside); construction underway 2024-26, first CO2 injection targeted ~2027-28.

name: Liverpool Bay CCS / HyNet T&S (Eni, Irish Sea)
source: Seed data; Eni HyNet updates 2025-26
notes: Depleted gas fields (Hamilton, Douglas) in Liverpool Bay, Irish Sea, repurposed for CO2 storage; part of UK Track-1 HyNet cluster; construction/conversion of platforms ongoing 2025-26, first injection targeted 2027.

name: Viking CCS (Harbour Energy/bp, Southern North Sea)
source: Seed data; UK Track-2 process 2025-26
notes: Depleted gas fields off Lincolnshire coast (Viking field cluster), targeting up to 10 Mtpa long-term. Selected in UK Track-2 cluster sequencing; FEED/investment decisions progressing through 2025-26.

name: Bifrost CCS Phase 1 (TotalEnergies/DUC, Harald West & Dagny, Denmark)
source: Seed data; TotalEnergies Denmark updates
notes: Depleted Harald and Dagny fields, Danish North Sea; second major Danish offshore CO2 storage project alongside Greensand; targeting cross-border CO2 imports from Belgium/Germany.

name: Equinor Smeaheia CO2 Storage (North Sea, Norway)
source: Seed data; Equinor Smeaheia project updates
notes: Large saline aquifer storage license adjacent to Northern Lights/Troll area, North Sea; PDO progressing 2025-26 to complement Northern Lights capacity growth.

name: Ravenna CCS Phase 1 (Eni/Snam, Adriatic)
source: Seed data (Eni/Snam Sep 2024 PR; OnePetro OMC-2025-675)
notes: Italy's first CCS project; Phase 1 operational since Aug 2024, injecting into Porto Corsini Mare Ovest depleted gas reservoir. Phase 2 targeting ~4 Mtpa by 2030, expansion vision to 16 Mtpa Mediterranean hub.

name: Guohua Jinjie CCS (China Energy, Shaanxi)
source: Seed data; Global CCS Institute China facilities database
notes: Coal-power CCS demonstration in Shaanxi Province, saline aquifer storage; separate from China Energy's Ordos CCUS demonstration.

name: QatarEnergy Ras Laffan CCS
source: Seed data; Global CCS Institute; QatarEnergy sustainability reports
notes: CO2 captured from LNG trains at Ras Laffan Industrial City injected into onshore saline aquifer; being expanded as part of QatarEnergy's plan to reach 11 Mtpa CCS capacity by 2030s tied to North Field expansion.

name: Bayou Bend CCS (SE Texas, Gulf Coast)
source: Seed data; Chevron/Talos updates 2025-26
notes: Large saline storage hub, Jefferson/Chambers Counties, Texas Gulf Coast; multiple Class VI applications in EPA/Texas RRC review pipeline 2025-26 following Texas primacy grant (Nov 2025).

name: Summit Carbon Solutions Midwest Carbon Express (US Midwest Ethanol)
source: Inside Climate News Mar 2026 'Summit Sold Its Midwest Pipeline as a Carbon Solution. Now It'll Be Used for Fossil Fuels'; PGJ Apr 2026; South Dakota Searchlight
notes: ~2,500-mile pipeline linking ~57 Midwest ethanol plants (IA/MN/NE/ND/SD) to North Dakota sequestration site. MAJOR 2026 STATUS CHANGE: North Dakota judge revoked Summit's underground CO2 storage permit in March 2026; South Dakota banned eminent domain for CO2 pipelines (2025); company pivoted messaging toward enhanced oil recovery (EOR) use rather than pure dedicated storage, undercutting original sequestration framing. Sequestration component now uncertain/contested as of mid-2026 — flagged as at-risk/EOR-adjacent.

name: Alberta Carbon Trunk Line (ACTL)
source: Seed data; Wolf Midstream/Enhance updates
notes: Captures CO2 from NWR Sturgeon Refinery and Nutrien Redwater Fertilizer, transports via trunk line to Clive/central Alberta oilfields — primarily EOR use currently (flagged EOR-adjacent), ~1.6 Mtpa transported vs. 14.6 Mtpa pipeline capacity; included per task instructions for status tracking.

name: Aquistore CO2 Storage Project (Saskatchewan)
operator: Whitecap Resources / PTRC (Petroleum Technology Research Centre)
source: Seed data; PTRC Aquistore program updates
notes: Dedicated deep saline aquifer storage (Deadwood/Winnipeg Fm ~3,400m) near Estevan, SK, receiving CO2 from SaskPower Boundary Dam CCS via pipeline (alongside EOR deliveries to Weyburn field); one of the few purely dedicated (non-EOR) saline injection sites in Canada.

name: Wabash Valley Resources CCS (Illinois Basin, Indiana)
source: Indiana Capital Chronicle Jan 2026 & Jun 2026; EPA final permit Jan 2024
notes: Ammonia fertilizer plant (former coal gasification site, West Terre Haute) storing CO2 in Mt. Simon Sandstone. EPA issued final Class VI permit Jan 2024, still under appeal before the Environmental Appeals Board as of 2026; Indiana pursuing state Class VI primacy (announced Jan 2026); local community opposition ongoing (per Jun 2026 coverage).

name: Stratos DAC + CCS (1PointFive/Occidental, Ector County, Texas)
source: 1PointFive/Oxy Apr 2025 Class VI permit PR; OGJ; Carbon Capture Magazine
notes: World's largest DAC facility (500kt/yr capacity design, up to 1 Mtpa scalable), Penwell TX. EPA issued 3 Class VI permits Apr 2025 (BRP CCS1-3 wells, ~722,000 t/yr saline storage capacity at ~4,400 ft depth) — first-ever Class VI permits issued for a DAC project. Became commercially operational 2025; amendments to permits under public comment (deadline Jul 2026).

name: South Texas DAC Hub (King Ranch, 1PointFive/Occidental/ADNOC XRG)
source: Oxy.com JV press release; Carbon Credits; Natural Gas Intel
notes: 165 sq-mile King Ranch site, Kleberg County TX; DOE award up to $650M; Occidental and ADNOC's XRG agreed to evaluate a $500M joint venture (2025-26) to develop the hub. First DAC facility (500kt/yr) in front-end engineering & design (FEED); site claimed to have up to 3 Gt total storage potential across the full hub footprint (long-term, multi-facility ceiling, not near-term capacity).

name: Bluebonnet Hub (1PointFive, Texas Gulf Coast)
source: 1PointFive news release 'Plan to Develop a Carbon Capture and Sequestration Hub in Southeast Texas'
notes: 55,000+ acre CO2 storage hub spanning Chambers, Liberty and Jefferson counties, TX, near Beaumont-Houston refining/chemical corridor; storing third-party captured CO2 in saline formations (not tied to O&G production); targeted operational 2026, permitting ongoing.

name: ExxonMobil Jefferson County CCS (Baytown/Southeast Texas)
source: EPA newsrelease 'EPA Issues Three Class VI Permits to ExxonMobil in Jefferson County, Texas' Oct 2025
notes: 3 Class VI permits issued Oct 21, 2025, each authorizing 1.1-1.67 Mt/yr injection (5 Mtpa combined across 3 wells), up to 53 Mt total over 13-year injection period. Complements ExxonMobil Baytown low-carbon hydrogen + CCS hub and broader Gulf Coast network (largest US CO2 pipeline owner post-Denbury acquisition, ~1,300 miles).

name: ExxonMobil/Denbury Virgo CO2 Storage Site (Louisiana)
source: Rextag; OGJ 'ExxonMobil signs land deal for largest CO2 offshore storage site'
notes: 8,500-acre dedicated saline sequestration site ~50 miles NE of Baton Rouge, LA; estimated storage capacity of at least 100 Mt CO2; targeted to accept first injection as early as 2026, part of ExxonMobil's ~100 Mt Gulf Coast network ambition.

name: ExxonMobil Baytown Low-Carbon Hydrogen + CCS (Texas)
source: Seed data; ExxonMobil corporate updates 2025-26
notes: World's largest low-carbon hydrogen project with integrated CCS; would capture ~98% of CO2 from hydrogen production (~7 Mtpa) for saline storage in Gulf Coast reservoirs; construction/engineering ongoing 2025-26.

name: Project Tundra (Minnkota Power, North Dakota)
source: Seed data; Minnkota updates
notes: Coal-power CCS retrofit at Milton R. Young Station, storing CO2 in Broom Creek Fm saline aquifer, ND (state Class VI primacy jurisdiction); FEED/financing progressing 2025-26.

name: Red Trail Energy CCS (Richardton, North Dakota)
source: Seed data; ND Industrial Commission records
notes: Ethanol-plant CO2 injected into Broom Creek Fm saline aquifer under ND state Class VI primacy; one of the first operational dedicated saline storage sites under state (non-EPA) permitting.

name: CarbonTerraVault I (Elk Hills, Kern County, California)
source: GlobeNewswire May 26 2026 'CRC Achieves First CO2 Injection at Carbon TerraVault I'; Hart Energy
notes: California's first operational CCS project; first CO2 injection achieved May 2026 into the 26R depleted reservoir at CRC's Elk Hills Field. EPA issued California's first Class VI permits Dec 31, 2024. CTV I-26R: 1.46 Mtpa capacity, 38 Mt total storage capacity. CRC has 8 additional CTV reservoirs (~352 Mt combined) submitted for permitting; CTV VII (~27 Mt) permit application submitted Feb 2026.

name: PureField Carbon Capture (Russell, Kansas)
source: EPA newsrelease 'EPA Issues Class VI Well Permit to PureField Carbon Capture in Kansas' Apr 10 2026
notes: EPA Region 7 issued final Class VI permit Apr 10 2026 for ethanol-plant CO2 storage near Russell, KS; authorized to inject up to 150,000 t/yr for 12 years (1.8 Mt total permitted volume).

name: Shell Polaris CCS / Atlas Carbon Storage Hub (Scotford, Alberta)
source: Seed data; Shell Canada updates 2025-26
notes: Expansion of Shell's Scotford-area CCS (companion to Quest) targeting additional industrial CO2 volumes in the Atlas storage hub, Basal Cambrian Sands saline aquifer; construction progressing 2025-26.

name: Pathways Alliance CCS Hub (Cold Lake, Alberta)
operator: Pathways Alliance (Canadian Natural, Cenovus, ConocoPhillips, Imperial, MEG, Suncor)
source: IEEFA 'Pathways CCS Project Won't Break Even'; NRCan; Carbon Herald 2026
notes: Proposed pipeline network linking 13 oil sands processing facilities to a storage hub near Cold Lake, AB. No final investment decision as of mid-2026; IEEFA financial-risk analysis (2025-26) questions project economics absent greater public subsidy; status remains pre-FID/planned despite years of development work.

name: Enbridge Open Access Wabamun Carbon Hub (Alberta)
source: Seed data; Enbridge updates 2025-26
notes: Open-access saline aquifer storage hub west of Edmonton targeting multiple industrial emitters; pore space leases and characterization ongoing.

name: Aramco Jubail CCS Hub
operator: Saudi Aramco (EPC: Larsen & Toubro; FEED: Wood)
source: JPT SPE; CCUS Hub OGCI; hydrogen-worldexpo.com 2026; MEES
notes: Flagship Saudi CCS hub targeting 9 Mtpa capacity by 2027 (6 Mtpa Aramco-allocated, 3 Mtpa for third-party/non-Aramco facilities), part of Saudi Arabia interim 44 Mtpa-by-2035 sequestration target. 2025 marked transition to execution: .5B EPC contract to Larsen & Toubro for CO2 processing/compression facilities; >.7B in contracts deployed in 2025. First phase includes 200+ km dense-phase CO2 pipeline network. On track for 2027 commissioning as of mid-2026.

name: South Africa Pilot CCUS Research Site (Leandra, Mpumalanga)
source: Engineering News Sep 2024; ESI-Africa; Council for Geoscience press release
notes: South Africa's first CCUS research/pilot site, USM program, inaugurated Sep 2024 near Leandra, Mpumalanga (close to Sasol and coal-fired power plant CO2 sources). Zululand and Algoa basins were considered but Leandra (Mpumalanga) selected for Phase 1 characterization due to source proximity; site holds theoretical capacity noted at up to 34 Gt CO2 (basin-scale figure, not a project-scale target). Phase 1 (characterization) concluded 2024; Phase 2 (design/construction/injection) not yet underway as of mid-2026 status.
